principle: Archer is a pilot used to prove standards. Do not copy pilot bureaucracy to every unit; reuse proven standards and escalate only exceptions.
fastPathEligibilityAllRequired: the motion archetype pipeline has already passed at least one accepted pilot or representative package
the unit has an exact approved current source/neutral identity
the requested motion is part of the current production priority rather than optional polish
no unique marker complexity or ambiguous semantic event is present
no unusual anatomy requires a new motion method
the unit/package has no unresolved identity-drift, near-static, checker/background or repeated rejection pattern
a reusable common validator plus the correct motion-specific gate supports the package
standard automatic review artifacts can prove the package clearly at board/mobile scale
package paths, hashes, approval and rollback can remain isolated from batch siblings
no runtime/Core Logic/gameplay change is required to support this package
fastPathWorkflow: Exact-File Approval Overlay for Passed Packages
Grouped Runtime Migration for Approved Packages
fastPathSkipsByDefault: separate technical-handoff PR when the proven batch template already contains all required fields
one PR per simple motion package
re-proving the same runtime architecture already accepted by the pilot

slowPathTriggers.trigger: identity drift or current master mismatch
slowPathTriggers.action: stop batch candidate and resolve source identity individually
individual motion redesign/review; do not let technical PASS override failure
individual or very small biomechanics-specific batch
individual motion contract and marker review

slowPathTriggers.trigger: special VFX timing coupled to a semantic pose
slowPathTriggers.action: individual marker/visual contract; keep VFX runtime-owned

slowPathTriggers.trigger: unusual anatomy or first member of a new locomotion archetype
slowPathTriggers.action: representative archetype pilot before wider batching

slowPathTriggers.trigger: marker ambiguity or legacy marker conflict
slowPathTriggers.action: derive timing from actual visual event and current gameplay contract
isolate package and require dedicated diagnostics/review
invalidate affected derivative fast-path assumptions and version/redo as needed
separate CC runtime scope; do not hide inside an asset batch

returnFromSlowPathToFastPath: a representative package passes technical + visual + exact approval
the new anatomy/weapon/motion rule is encoded as reusable config/gate
at least one later sibling candidate can reuse the proven method without copying identity/pose blindly

batchApprovalRules.failedPackageAction: Remove from approval set, create isolated redo/version, retain passed package bytes unchanged.

simpleFastPath.production: One batch production PR for several isolated compatible packages.
simpleFastPath.approval: One batch approval overlay PR may lock several explicitly approved packages with separate package records/hashes.
simpleFastPath.runtime: One grouped runtime migration PR may import several approved packages with per-unit/state rollback.

prOptimization.slowPath: Complex/unique/failed packages use individual PRs only where the risk justifies isolation.
prOptimization.auditRule: PR grouping reduces coordination overhead but never merges package identities, hashes or rollback boundaries.

reviewFailureIsolation.example: If 5 Idle packages are reviewed together and 1 fails, approve the 4 passed exact packages and redo only the failed one if ancestry/package paths remain independent.
reviewFailureIsolation.forbidden: Editing already-passed sibling package bytes merely to keep the batch visually uniform.

CC: implement reusable validator/artifact tooling when separately authorized
Coco: prepare exact-file approval records after explicit user decisions
ownership.noDuplication: Coco does not generate production motion or modify runtime while CC owns execution.
